summary:
Tumours over 4cm, inflammatory characteristics, chest wall involvement all indicate locally advanced disease. Skin involvement may suggest concern but isn’t sufficient without deeper tissue extension to be classified definitively as such. \boxed{D}

The summary accurately captures the critical distinctions between various indicators while focusing solely on clinically pertinent aspects relative to classifying a condition as 'locally advanced' versus other options presented. Agent2’s initial response was thorough yet could have been more concise; this refined version ensures clarity within two succinct statements. The conclusion clearly identifies why option D stands out based on typical medical standards regarding loco-regional staging classifications.
